title: NCLT relied on "hallucinated citations" in its judgment: Suspended Essel director tells Supreme Court
description: A suspended director of Essel Infraprojects told the Supreme Court on Tuesday that the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) at Mumbai relied on "hallucinated" non-existent Supreme Court judgments/ citations in its order admitting Essel Infraprojects corporate insolvency resolution process (CIRP) [Pooja Ramesh Singh Vs Jammu & Kashmir Bank].Senior Advocate Madhavi Divan, appearing for suspended director Pooja Ramesh Singh, told a Bench of Justices PS Narasimha and Alok Aradhe that the NCLT ha
